coreTruthSet.description: Natural levees are presented as precision settlement zones balancing flood avoidance, water access, and fertile soils.
Oxbow lakes such as Cane River are described as settlement anchors due to stable water and reduced exposure relative to main channels.
coreTruthSet.quoteExcerpt: Oxbow lakes... former river channels... stable water sources... ideal for settlement... safer than main river flow.

coreTruthSet.description: The Red River is framed as a governing system of movement, boundaries, exchange, and information flow rather than passive background.
coreTruthSet.quoteExcerpt: The Red River is not scenery. It is: The road, the boundary, the marketplace, the messenger.

coreTruthSet.description: Cane brakes are treated as defensive and mobility infrastructure: barriers externally, pathways for those with local knowledge.
coreTruthSet.quoteExcerpt: Cane brakes function as natural barriers, defensive shields, hidden pathways (if you know them).

coreTruthSet.description: Power durability is tied to ecological literacy: those who read water movement and site correctly outlast competitors.
coreTruthSet.quoteExcerpt: Who understands where to build and where not to build survives longer than everyone else.

settingAnchors.name: Cane River Oxbow Anchor
settingAnchors.description: Stable off-channel settlement anchor balancing access to routes with lower direct exposure to main-flow volatility.

settingAnchors.name: Natural Levee Settlement Band
settingAnchors.description: Slightly elevated alluvial ground used for durable village and field placement under recurring flood pressure.

settingAnchors.name: Red River Mobility Corridor
settingAnchors.description: Primary movement artery linking settlement logic, exchange, and strategic positioning in the chapter’s geography-of-power frame.

continuityConstraints.title: Main Channel vs Oxbow Dual Logic
continuityConstraints.summary: Core spatial tension constraint for chapter architecture.
continuityConstraints.text: Main river channels prioritize movement and exposure; oxbow zones prioritize continuity and control. Strong chapters stage tension between these two geographies.

continuityConstraints.title: Cane Brake Visibility Control
continuityConstraints.text: Cane brakes function as selective visibility infrastructure: concealment, defense, and controlled mobility for informed actors.

continuityConstraints.title: Hydrological Literacy as Power
continuityConstraints.text: Political survival follows hydrological literacy: reading bends, floods, narrows, and branches determines who can route people, goods, and risk.

openResearchGaps.title: Who held actionable knowledge of cane-brake routes and how was that knowledge controlled or transmitted?
openResearchGaps.description: Needed for plausible power asymmetry in movement, defense, and secrecy scenes.

openResearchGaps.title: What measurable risk differences existed between main-channel and oxbow-adjacent settlement patterns?
openResearchGaps.description: Helps calibrate scene stakes and migration/siting decisions beyond qualitative framing.

openResearchGaps.title: Which specific time slices show major Red River course shifts most relevant to Grappe-era settlement decisions?
openResearchGaps.description: Need periodized river-change anchors to avoid flattening hydrological dynamics across generations.
